LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS MATTHEW GRAFFINGNA, TIMOTHY GRAFFINGNA, MARK VASQUEZ, KRISTEN VASQUEZ, AND PETER MIKACICH'S ANTI-SLAPP SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT WAS TAKEN UNDER SUBMISSION. ---------- AFTER HEARING, THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED - DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE 7TH - 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE DEFENDANTS CONDUCT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS NOT CONSIDERED PROTECTED PRE-LITIGATION OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CLARK V. MAZGANI (2009) 170 CAL.APP.4TH 1281. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,125.00 IS GRANTED. GIVEN THAT THERE WAS CASE LAW DIRECTLY ON POINT, THE COURT DOES FIND THAT THIS MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS. PLAINTIFF TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-16-2011 Continued To Sep-19-11 At 01:30 P.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Anti-Slapp Motion To Strike Pltf'S First Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS MATTHEW GRAFFINGNA, TIMOTHY GRAFFINGNA, MARK VASQUEZ, KRISTEN VASQUEZ, AND PETER MIKACICH'S ANTI-SLAPP SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE PLAINTIFF'S FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Mary Catherine Wiederhold, Esq. 533-0735 for pltfs & opposing parties; Curtis E. Smolar, Esq. of Ropers Majeski Kohn Bentley 972-6308 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court took the matter under submission.
After hearing, the Court ruled as follows: Tentative ruling adopted - DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE THE 7TH - 11TH CAUSES OF ACTION OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS DENIED. THE DEFENDANTS CONDUCT THAT IS THE SUBJECT OF PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT IS NOT CONSIDERED PROTECTED PRE-LITIGATION OR PETITIONING ACTIVITY. CLARK V. MAZGANI (2009) 170 CAL.APP.4TH 1281. PLAINTIFFS' REQUEST FOR FEES IN THE AMOUNT OF $4,125.00 IS GRANTED. GIVEN THAT THERE WAS CASE LAW DIRECTLY ON POINT, THE COURT DOES FIND THAT THIS MOTION IS FRIVOLOUS. Plaintiff to prepare a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489
